scope: Lane 2 -- raise the VRAM ceiling so the 640x320 1km grid fits. Root-cause the un-tiled full-grid intermediate the km-bench attributed the 32 GiB ceiling to.

given_evidence.peak_vram_fit: LINEAR in ncol: 0.165 GiB / 1000 col (R2 0.997); intercept ~ -1.5 GiB
given_evidence.first_oom: 209,880 cols (264x795): a SINGLE allocation of 28.44 GiB, with a 19.08 GiB resident MaxAllocSize
given_evidence.target_640x320: 204,800-205,761 cols -> projected ~32.3 GiB -> does NOT fit 32 GiB
given_evidence.target_560x280_small: 156,800 cols -> ~24.4 GiB -> FITS (already spans the whole 7-island Canary archipelago at 1km, per km_feasibility_verdict.json)

root_cause_correction.brief_premise: the ceiling is ONE un-chunked full-grid radiation/PBL intermediate not capped by the existing 16384-col tiling
root_cause_correction.finding: PARTIALLY REFUTED. Source audit shows BOTH the radiation working set AND the MYNN BouLac dense matrices ARE already column-tiled, so neither is the un-capped full-grid term:

radiation_is_tiled.lw: rrtmg_lw_theta_tendency -> solve_rrtmg_lw_column -> _longwave_column_tiled_impl (src/gpuwrf/physics/rrtmg_lw.py:2627) runs the ENTIRE _longwave_impl (incl MCICA cloud generation) inside a lax.scan over 16384-col tiles; knobs GPUWRF_RRTMG_LW_COLUMN_TILING/_COLUMN_TILE_COLS default on/16384
radiation_is_tiled.v013_proof: proofs/v013/rrtmg_column_tile_vram_suite.json: LW untiled @65536col OOMs at 32 GiB but tiled = 5.4 GiB; SW untiled 10 GiB -> tiled 1.6 GiB. The tiled radiation working set is a FIXED ~5-6 GiB (per 16384-col tile), NOT linear in ncol.

mynn_boulac_is_tiled.where: step_mynn_pbl_column -> _tiled_mynn_step (src/gpuwrf/physics/mynn_pbl.py:1395) runs _step_mynn_pbl_impl_with_pblh (which builds the dense (B,nz,nz) _boulac_length matrices) inside a lax.scan over 16384-col tiles; knob GPUWRF_MYNN_COLUMN_TILING/_TILE_COLS default on/16384
mynn_boulac_is_tiled.consequence: at the 640x320 target the per-tile BouLac transient is (16384, 50, 50) = 0.305 GiB x ~6 live ~= 1.8 GiB, NOT the 23 GiB the un-tiled empirical_memory_map rank-3 estimate would imply

actual_ceiling_composition.verdict: The 0.165 GiB/1000col linear growth is the SUM of the many full-grid (ncol,nz) arrays that are NOT column-tiled -- the prognostic State (~30 leaves x 0.077 GiB @target = ~2.3 GiB), the acoustic/dycore work arrays, the per-RK-stage tendencies, and the UN-TILED column-physics adapters -- NOT one un-chunked giant. The reported '28.44 GiB single allocation' at OOM is XLA's fused-module temp arena / largest live coalesced region, dominated by these full-ncol arrays held simultaneously.

largest_untiled_physics_scheme.name: microphysics adapters (scan_adapters.py / physics_couplers.py)
largest_untiled_physics_scheme.evidence: proofs/v014/empirical_memory_map.json candidate 'non_radiation_column_physics_tiling' (rank 4): the MP adapters run on the WHOLE (ncol,nz) batch with NO column tiling (_mp_in -> kernel -> _apply_mp_replacements); 8-15 full-domain input leaves + 6 replacement leaves ~= 1.0-2.0 GiB at the target geometry, un-tiled

fit_to_target.saving_needed_to_28GiB: ~4-5 GiB off the projected 32.3 GiB peak
fit_to_target.actionable_bit_identical_path: column-tile the microphysics adapter with the SAME lax.scan-over-leading-column-tiles pattern already proven for RRTMG (proofs/v013/rrtmg_column_tile.py) and MYNN (_tiled_mynn_step). A 16384-col MP tile caps its full-grid I/O+working set to ~1 tile, removing the ~1-2 GiB MP term AND its scheme-internal temporaries; combined with the two FIX_NOW_BIT_IDENTICAL items (~0.3-0.7 GiB) this is the cleanest bit-identical route toward the ~4-5 GiB needed. Chunking is exact (bit-identical), gate = CPU tile-vs-untiled exact-output per scheme (empirical_memory_map required_proof_gate).
fit_to_target.why_not_implemented_this_sprint: HONEST DEFERRAL. (1) The single-biggest lever (MP column-tiling) is a real multi-scheme implementation needing per-scheme exact-output gates + a >=160k-col before/after VRAM suite -- not a knob-flip. (2) The GPU box degraded mid-sprint into a persistent stepping/finalize stall (~900 ms/step launch-bound throttle + a finite_summary jit stall on the full operational pipeline) that blocked every large-grid GPU run I attempted, so I could neither re-measure the 209k OOM buffer nor validate a chunking change's before/after VRAM. Implementing chunking blind + unverifiable would violate the 'profiler/VRAM artifact for every claim' rule. (3) The 560x280 SMALL target -- which already spans the whole archipelago at 1km -- FITS today (24.4 GiB); only the denser 640x320 footprint needs this work.
fit_to_target.recommendation: next sprint, on a clean GPU: (a) run proofs/perf/v015/km_bench/vram_buffer_probe.py (added this sprint; needs the canairy run dir present in the worktree) to name the exact 28.44 GiB region by HLO instruction+shape, then (b) column-tile the microphysics adapter (rank-4) + land the two FIX_NOW_BIT_IDENTICAL items, with a >=160k-col before/after peak-VRAM suite as the gate.
